title: arthrophyllum hederoides
intro: Arthrophyllum hederoides, also known as the mountain fern or New Zealand tree fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. It is a shrub-like plant that grows in the mountainous regions of New Zealand and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and has also been found to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Arthrophyllum hederoides plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
